---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="page-header container">
  <p class="page-header-label">Education</p>
  <h1 class="page-header-title">Teaching</h1>
  <p class="page-header-sub">
    Data storytelling and applied research methods for professionals.
  </p>
  <p style="color:#6b7280; font-size:0.95rem; margin-top:0.75rem;">
    Recently presented <em>Telling Your Court's Data Story</em> at the
    National Association for Court Management (2025).
  </p>
</div>
```

## How I teach {.section-heading}

I teach with real datasets, flaws and all. Real data has gaps, debatable
scores, and choices somebody made but never wrote down. That is what people
face at work, so that is what we practice on.

The six ideas I come back to most often:

::: {.concept-list}
<span class="concept-pill" style="background:#f3edf3; border-color:#8C6A8D; color:#6b4f6c;">Construct validity</span>
<span class="concept-pill" style="background:#fdf0ec; border-color:#E98973; color:#c96b52;">Sampling and bias</span>
<span class="concept-pill" style="background:#f8f5f0; border-color:#D2B1A3; color:#2B2D42;">Multidimensional measurement</span>
<span class="concept-pill" style="background:#f3edf3; border-color:#6b4f6c; color:#6b4f6c;">Uncertainty communication</span>
<span class="concept-pill" style="background:#fdf0ec; border-color:#c96b52; color:#c96b52;">Visualization design</span>
<span class="concept-pill" style="background:#F8F7F4; border-color:#888; color:#333;">Narrative framing</span>
:::

---

## Teaching materials {.section-heading}

### The Hip-Hop Periodic Table: Data Storytelling Module

A complete 90-minute session built around a dataset of 173 hip-hop acts,
each scored on five parts of lyricism. Designed for professional and
graduate audiences.

**Covers:** all six ideas above.

**The materials:**

- [Interactive periodic table](../hiphop/hiphop_periodic_table.html): the branded, filterable artist table
- [Analytics dashboard](../hiphop/hiphop_dashboard.html): a companion Quarto dashboard with charts
- [Data dictionary](../hiphop/data_dictionary.html): what each score means, how scores were assigned, how much to trust them, and known biases
- Excel workbook: the full dataset (Artist Database, Confidence Guide, and Data Dictionary sheets)

::: {.callout-tip title="Session design note"}
The session opens with a quick exercise: rate a favorite artist's lyricism
on a scale of 1 to 10, right away, no second-guessing. Then comes the real
question. What did you just measure? Working that out takes a good 45
minutes, and by the end people are debating construct validity using
their own performance measures as examples.
:::

---

## Topics I teach and write about {.section-heading}

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Measurement and metrics**

- Building performance measures that hold up
- When composite scores help and when they mislead
- Communicating confidence and uncertainty to non-technical stakeholders
- The politics of how organizational metrics get designed
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Data visualization**

- Choosing chart types that match your argument
- Designing for uncertainty, not false precision
- Interactive vs. static, and when to use each
- Common visualization mistakes in stakeholder reporting
:::
::::
